deploy/charts.purpose: Organisational parent for the two self-contained Helm charts (benthos-collector, openmeter) that package all OpenMeter Kubernetes workloads. Owns only the shared helm-docs generation pipeline (Makefile + template.md) consumed by both child charts; no shared K8s manifests, CRDs, or cluster-scoped RBAC live at this level.

patterns.name: helm-docs via shared template
patterns.description: Both child chart READMEs are generated by helm-docs from the shared template.md defined here. Run `make docs` to regenerate; never hand-edit a chart README.md.

patterns.name: OCI Helm registry distribution
patterns.description: Charts are packaged with `helm package deploy/charts/<CHART> --version <VERSION> --destination build/helm` and pushed to ghcr.io/openmeterio/helm-charts/. Chart.yaml version and appVersion stay aligned with the container image tag.

patterns.name: Self-contained child charts
patterns.description: Each child chart (benthos-collector, openmeter) is fully self-contained with its own templates/, values.yaml, Chart.yaml, and README.tmpl.md. No shared manifests live at deploy/charts level.

key_file_guides.role: Runs helm-docs across both child charts using the shared template.md. The sole target is `make docs`.
key_file_guides.watch_for: helm-docs must be on PATH (it is in the Nix CI shell); running outside Nix silently produces no output if helm-docs is missing.

key_file_guides.role: Shared Go-template for helm-docs defining badge helpers (versionBadge, typeBadge, appVersionBadge, kubeVersionBadge, artifactHubBadge) and the tldr / chart.base layout used by both child README templates.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Editing badge/section templates affects both child READMEs. artifactHubBadge URL is derived from .Name — renaming a chart breaks its ArtifactHub listing.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing a child chart README.md — generated by helm-docs from template.md and values.yaml comments; edits are overwritten on next `make docs`
Adding a third chart at this level without a corresponding README.tmpl.md and updating the Makefile docs target
Storing shared Kubernetes manifests (CRDs, cluster-scoped RBAC) at deploy/charts level — each chart must be fully self-contained
Publishing charts without aligning Chart.yaml appVersion with the container image tag used in values.yaml
Adding optional infrastructure to the openmeter chart without both an enabled guard in the resource template AND a matching override block in configmap.yaml

decisions.decision: Shared template.md at deploy/charts level rather than duplicated per chart
decisions.rationale: Both charts share the same badge set and TL;DR install pattern; centralising ensures consistent branding and eliminates duplication across helm-docs runs.

decisions.decision: OCI registry (ghcr.io) as the distribution channel, not a traditional Helm repo index
decisions.rationale: OCI registries are natively supported by Helm 3+ and reuse the same GHCR credentials as container images, simplifying the release pipeline.

decisions.decision: All OpenMeter binaries share one image and tag in the openmeter chart
decisions.rationale: The mono-repo produces all binaries from a single Dockerfile and release tag; sharing one image tag enforces version consistency and prevents partial upgrades (only Svix is exempt with its own image fields).

code_examples.scenario: Add a new OpenMeter workload to the openmeter chart
